name: Zucchini-Shrimp Coquilles
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease the bottom of a deep skillet with butter.
2. Add shrimp, zucchini, white wine, water, fennel seed, and lemon juice to the skillet. Cover and bring to boiling point over medium heat.
3. Turn the shrimp over and continue cooking 1 minute longer over medium heat, covered.
4. Using a slotted spoon, remove the shrimp and zucchini from the skillet and set aside.
5. Reduce the liquid in the skillet by 3/4 cup over high heat without covering. This should take about 5 minutes.
6. Mix in Bercy sauce, salt, pepper, and tabasco. Cook for 1-2 minutes over medium heat.
7. Return the shrimp and zucchini to the sauce and mix well.
8. Spoon the mixture into scallop shells and top with breadcrumbs. Broil in the oven until hot.
